results.title: STOP using ChatGPT (use private AI alternatives)
results.content:
floating around proton, Lumo is the easiest way I've found to meaningfully reduce AI data
risk while still getting a functional useful AI product. There's a chance that a year
from now all of the products and options that I just
mentioned will be gone or completely replaced. That is how fast AI is moving, but that's also why it's
more important than ever to consider your approach to
ai, how you decide which tools that you're gonna use as opposed to focusing just on the tools themselves. So let me give you a quick framework I'm using to think about ai. Regardless of all the
new models, new tools and new agents that are
being announced every day, here are the eight rules I
think are worth following. When you interact with ai. First, the forever assumption, [...] do that deep clean for you. Over the past few years,
DeleteMe has saved me hundreds of hours of time by locating, removing, and continuing to scan for
all of my sensitive data. I've got my family
using DeleteMe as well, and it's something I recommend for anybody who is serious about privacy and cleaning up their digital footprint. Get 20% off when you visit, joindeleteme.com/allthingssecured and they even serve those of
you who are outside of the us. If you use the International link that I'll leave in the description. Proton, yes, you already
know I'm a big fan of their other privacy
products like Proton Mail and Proton Pass, and now
they have Lumo, an AI product that feels like it was
built for normal people who want better privacy without [...] it's not front and center and the privacy explanations
aren't especially obvious unless you go looking for them. During testing, I also
ran into a few small but noticeable quirks. Some responses stalled out
on me or cut off completely and a few UI behaviors felt
unfinished or slightly buggy. Nothing deal breaking, but enough to remind you that
we're still in the early years of AI and it wasn't a polished
consumer first product yet. So here's how I'd frame it. Tools like minstrel a chat
are powerful, flexible, and impressively fast
without relying on the biggest AI companies. But if privacy is your priority, you need to actively verify the settings and understand what you're opting out of. But what if you don't want
your data leaving your computer at all? That is the extreme end

results.title: What alternative AI platform do you prefer to use?
results.content:
Author has 89 answers and 11.8K answer views

·May 6

There are so many ai alternative platforms out right now so it’s hard to settle on one. For ai image generation I’d recommend cantina compared to grok as it’s free and gives pretty quality generations for not being a paid version. For writing I’d say Gemini over chat as it’s pretty similar but Gemini is better in some aspects in my opinion.

Upvote · 

Image 4: Profile photo for Vod Kanockers

Vod Kanockers

High Tech Subject Matter Expert (2002–present) · Author has 1.6K answers and 221.7K answer views

·Jan 26

I do not AI for anything. I don't have a need for it. My content is unique and created by me in the space I play in (engineering writing, technical seminars and high-tech consulting). [...] - Context across the whole project, not just one task.

AI that only sees one ticket at a time is too narrow to be useful. Look for tools where the intelligence works across your entire project, from related work, team dependencies, and historical patterns. Jira pulls context from across your connected tools and apps through the Atlassian Teamwork Graph, so the AI isn't just working from what's in the ticket. It's working from the full picture of your organization's work.

- From strategy to execution without losing fidelity.

The gap between "we need to do this" and "here's the actual backlog" is where projects stall. Good AI closes that gap to turn high-level goals into specific, assignable work.

- Human control at every step. [...] Continue Reading

The feature list on most tools looks similar. The difference shows up when you're three weeks into a project and the AI is either saving your team two hours a day, or sitting unused because it only works in theory.

Here's what separates tools that help from tools that look good in a demo:

- AI that reduces work, not just describes it. The bar isn't "can it write a summary." It's "does it take something off my team's plate." AI in Jira handles the tasks teams hate, like status updates and turning a strategy doc into actionable work. And with agents in Jira, it goes further. Rovo agents and third-party agents join your workflow as real participants, picking up work and making progress the same way a human teammate would.

results.title: 20+ Most Popular ChatGPT Alternatives - ClickHelp
results.content:
In response to these needs, companies began integrating ChatGPT with other AI tools that better aligned with their specific goals. This approach allowed them to leverage the strengths of multiple platforms, improving performance, personalization, and data privacy. In today’s article, we will explore some of the alternative AI tools that have helped companies meet their unique challenges while driving creativity and success in their respective fields.

> The world of AI tools is vast, and while ChatGPT leads in popularity, there are innovative alternatives that offer functionality designed to enhance documentation and other critical tasks. — Ian Corvin, Product Manager at ClickHelp

## Why Look for ChatGPT Alternatives? [...] + Jasper AI: A content creation and marketing tool that builds AI-powered content with different tones and styles.
  + Semrush ContentShake AI: A content ideation and optimization tool providing SEO-driven content suggestions and competitor analysis.
  + Surfer AI: SEO-empowered content creation where the analysis of SERPs is supported for search engine optimization.
 Conversational AI:
  + HuggingChat: Free open-source conversational AI with involvement from the community in the development and personalization of the models.
  + Character.AI: A platform for designing AI-driven virtual characters that engage in natural, dynamic conversations.
  + Chatsonic: Real-time data and AI conversation tool providing real-time news data, voice interactions, and image generation. [...] ## Conclusion

While ChatGPT remains a leading force in the world of AI tools, there are several other strong alternatives worth exploring, depending on your specific needs. Tools like ClickHelp’s WriteAssist and AnswerGenius excel at enhancing documentation and knowledge management, streamlining workflows, and boosting productivity. Additionally, platforms such as ClickUp, Jasper AI, and Google Gemini offer specialized features for project management, content creation, and multimodal AI experiences. For coding and development, GitHub Copilot and CodeWhisperer stand out, while others like Jasper and Writesonic shine in content generation and marketing.

results.title: I Tested 20+ ChatGPT Alternatives. These Are The Best in ...
results.content:
Ideal for: Microsoft 365 power users, enterprise teams, analysts, and professionals who want AI grounded in their emails, documents, and internal data.

Copilot is Microsoft’s way of bringing AI into the tools people already spend most of their day in. Instead of sitting in a separate chat window, it lives inside Word, Excel, Outlook, and the rest of the Microsoft 365 stack, helping you work through things as they come up.

Copilot works well for writing and spreadsheets, but that’s usually just where people start.

Once you spend time with it, you notice the smaller things. The built-in image generation(powered by DALL·E) is useful when you need a quick visual for a slide or a document. [...] ### Pricing

Copilot is bundled with Microsoft 365 plans. Pricing starts at $9.99/month for personal use. You can also find Business, Enterprise, and Copilot Studio plans.

How to Use Microsoft Copilot - 2026 Beginner's Guide

## 7. Meta AI: Best ChatGPT alternative for social & consumer use

Why I picked Meta AI: Instead of pulling you into a separate app, Meta AI shows up inside Facebook, WhatsApp, and other Meta products and helps in the middle of real conversations.

Ideal for: Everyday users, group chats, creators, and anyone who wants AI help for social planning, creative expression, and quick answers without leaving their messaging apps. [...] ### Pricing

Meta AI is free to use inside apps like WhatsApp, Instagram, and Facebook. There’s no separate subscription right now, and most features are available by default, depending on your region and device.

Zuckerberg Demos New Meta Glasses With Built-In Display

## 8. DeepSeek: Best ChatGPT alternative for technical reasoning & coding

Why I picked DeepSeek: DeepSeek feels built for thinking things through. It’s analytical, transparent, and unusually capable for a model that’s free and open source.

Ideal for: Developers, researchers, and technically inclined users who care about reasoning quality, math, coding accuracy, or running models locally without relying on cloud access.

results.title: Machine learning for product choice prediction | Journal of Marketing Analytics | Springer Nature Link
results.content:
In this paper, we compare a naïve multinomial logit product purchase predictive model against six other alternatives: learning multinomial logit, random forests, neural networks, gradient boosting, support vector machines and an ensemble learning algorithm. The results show that random forest and gradient boosting, in that order, perform best among the six single model approaches (excluding the ensemble model). However, some machine-learning approaches, namely neural networks and support vector machines, resulted in weaker predictive performance. In fact, both neural network and support vector machine approaches perform worse than even the naïve multinomial logit benchmark model, on average. However, neural network’s relative performance improves significantly with greater observations, [...] independently of their sample size. This result builds on existing evidence about the benefits of combining multiple prediction techniques over relying on a single best performing model, as conventional wisdom would intuitively make us believe. In general, the better performance of machine learning techniques at predicting product choice should not come as a surprise. At their core, machine learning techniques are designed to augment dimensionality of models and/or scan through orders of magnitude greater model alternatives, relative to the narrower focus of traditional approaches. [...] Step 2: For each model alternative

Step 2.1: Split the training data into a training subset and one or a number of validation-sets.

Step 2.2: Fit the model to the training subset data.

Step 2.3: Estimate performance of model against validation sets.

Step 3: After all model alternatives have been run, aggregate, and contrast results of all model alternatives.

Step 4: Select the best performing model, as shaped by a specific set of potential tuning parameters.

Step 5: Re-fit the best performing model on the entire training dataset to update model parameters.
